signals.reasoning: Cover the live SOL short now: it has round-tripped from +$27.33 peak unrealized to -$31.23 live, retains none of peak P&L, is flagged urgent, has no stop, and liquidation distance is under 1% while the active veto headlines are relief-style and directly hostile to the short thesis.
BTC remains structurally strong after the squeeze and is still holding near the highs; with the board under VETO, this is context for the SOL exit rather than a new decision to force.
ETH still shows trend-supported upside structure and does not offer a cleaner bearish expression than the live SOL position; under VETO this stays watch-only.
GRIFFAIN is already deep in long-liquidation-flush territory with negative funding, so chasing a fresh short here is lower quality than simply flattening the failed SOL risk.
BOME is noisy and incomplete on open-interest history, with a stretched rebound and no cleaner position-management edge than the existing SOL decision.
ZEC still looks trend-supported on price plus open interest, but the event veto makes it a watch-only name rather than something to fade or chase.
TST has a strong rebound profile but is already extended after the squeeze leg, so under VETO it is not clean enough to outrank the immediate SOL cleanup.
PNUT lacks enough open-interest history for a high-conviction mechanical read and is too thin and noisy to justify a fresh action during position management.
LDO still has constructive upside structure, but it is not a better use of attention than closing the compromised SOL short first.
XPL is strong but incomplete on the mechanical side and already extended; with VETO active it stays watch-only instead of actionable.
